reasoning: DeepSeek's comprehensive audit of PKGBUILD, build scripts, and source code found only legitimate package-query functionality. Qwen's inconclusive result stemmed from tool parsing failure, not security concerns.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen failed to parse its own SAFE decision for PKGBUILD, causing premature audit termination. DeepSeek completed full analysis with consistent safe verdicts.
coverage_issues: Qwen skipped all source files due to PKGBUILD parsing error
Both models omitted review of .install hooks (none present)
learnings: Improve XML response parsing robustness to prevent false inconclusive results
Prioritize source files with network/filesystem operations (e.g., aur.c, util.c) after PKGBUILD
Add fallback mechanism when model output parsing fails
Verify model consistency in handling standard AUR patterns (git describe, SKIP checksums)
